# Notebook Key Claims ‚Äî canonical numbers

*Last verified against executed notebook output: **2026-04-09**.*
*Generated by `notebooks-v2/update_key_claims.py`.*

## What this document is for

Every notebook in this project that ships load-bearing numbers to papers or external readers has a **Key Claims** block at its top, containing a table of canonical values with stable row IDs. This document aggregates all five blocks into one scannable file so paper writers don't have to open five notebooks to look up a value.

### The contract

- **If prose in a paper draft cites a value that disagrees with a row below, the paper is wrong ‚Äî not the notebook.** The notebook is the canonical source; the Key Claims block in the notebook is a direct transcription of its executed output; this file is a direct transcription of the Key Claims blocks.
- **If re-running a notebook produces different values**, update the in-notebook Key Claims block immediately, re-run `notebooks-v2/update_key_claims.py` to refresh this file, and `grep` for the old value across `docs/` and `docs/drafts/` to catch stale citations. Drafts are gitignored but still need the fix.
- **Stable IDs.** Papers cite rows as `[NB13:K5]`, `[NB11.5:K9]`, etc. Adding a new row gets a new K-ID; never renumber existing rows. If a claim is retired, replace its row body with *"(retired YYYY-MM-DD: reason)"* but keep the ID.

### Notebooks covered

- [NB13: `13_survey_phase`](#nb13-13_survey_phase) ‚Äî saccade amplitude phase distinction
- [NB11: `11_individual_differences`](#nb11-11_individual_differences) ‚Äî two-factor motor individual differences
- [NB11.5: `11_5_chattiness_traits`](#nb115-11_5_chattiness_traits) ‚Äî cursor chattiness as a stable individual-differences trait
- [NB14: `14_butterworth_cognitive_load`](#nb14-14_butterworth_cognitive_load) ‚Äî cognitive load decreases with SERP position
- [NB15: `15_cursor_approach`](#nb15-15_cursor_approach) ‚Äî cursor approach features and consideration set
- [NB21: `21_click_prediction`](#nb21-21_click_prediction) ‚Äî LOSO click prediction and four-class taxonomy
- [NB22: `22_four_class_taxonomy`](#nb22-22_four_class_taxonomy) ‚Äî regression-based four-class taxonomy and element-type interactions
- [NB23: `23_rank_effects`](#nb23-23_rank_effects) ‚Äî unified rank effects ‚Äî framework compilation

### Notebooks intentionally NOT covered

Only notebooks that ship numbers directly to external papers or public writeups get a Key Claims block. Internal exploratory notebooks, one-off investigations, and work-in-progress notebooks do not ‚Äî their numbers either aren't cited anywhere yet, or they change too frequently for the contract to hold. If you find a paper citing a notebook that isn't in the list above, that notebook needs a Key Claims block added via this script.

---

## NB13: `13_survey_phase` ‚Äî saccade amplitude phase distinction

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/13_survey_phase.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/13_survey_phase.ipynb)*

### Per-trial saccade amplitude slope (the main anchor result)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | N trials with ‚â• 10 saccades (unit of analysis for the slope test) | **2,754** |
| **K2** | Mean per-trial amplitude slope over first 20 saccades (negative = compression) | **œÅ = ‚àí0.135** (mean of per-trial Spearmans) |
| **K3** | One-sample *t*-test vs œÅ = 0 | *t* = ‚àí29.63, ***p* = 9.33 √ó 10‚Åª¬π‚Å∂‚Å∏**, *df* = 2,753 |
| **K4** | Fraction of trials with œÅ < 0 | 71.8% |

### Phase-level saccade amplitude medians (the phase distinction)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K5** | Survey-phase median saccade amplitude (fixations 1‚Äì5) | **107.8 px** (N = 13,840 saccades) |
| **K6** | Evaluate-phase median saccade amplitude (fixations 6+) | **69.4 px** (N = 65,764 saccades) |
| **K7** | Survey / Evaluate amplitude ratio | 1.55√ó |
| **K8** | Mann‚ÄìWhitney U, survey > evaluate | *p* ‚âà 0 (underflow; reported value 1.59 √ó 10‚Åª¬≤¬π‚Åπ on the re-windowed subset N = 9,550 / 45,262) |

### Other load-bearing rows

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K9** | Click rate given surveyed vs not-surveyed | 16.9% (N = 700) vs 11.9% (N = 10,368) |
| **K10** | Pre-scroll saccade amplitude median | 74.9 px (N = 59,343) |
| **K11** | Post-scroll saccade amplitude median | 67.2 px (N = 38,622) |
| **K12** | Pre-scroll > post-scroll (Mann‚ÄìWhitney) | *p* = 9.94 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å∂‚Å∂ |

> **Watch out:** stale drafts have cited N = 991, œÅ = ‚àí0.128, *p* = 1.5 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å∂¬π for K1/K2/K3. Those numbers are wrong by roughly 3√ó on N and by ~100 orders of magnitude on p. Use the values above. The 117 / 76 px pair some drafts cite for K5/K6 is also stale ‚Äî current values are 107.8 / 69.4 px.

<a id="nb11-11_individual_differences"></a>

## NB11: `11_individual_differences` ‚Äî two-factor motor individual differences

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/11_individual_differences.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/11_individual_differences.ipynb)*

### Panel summary (per-participant medians / means, n = 46 complete)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Median gaze‚Äìcursor lag (ms, negative = gaze leads cursor) | **‚àí650 ms** (Huang et al. 2012: ‚àí700 ms) |
| **K2** | Gaze‚Äìcursor lag range across participants | ‚àí1,825 to +925 ms (SD 572) |
| **K3** | Split-half reliability of gaze‚Äìcursor lag (Spearman‚ÄìBrown corrected) | **0.838** (raw *r* = 0.721, *n* = 46) |
| **K4** | Median TTI to first scroll | 5.46 s (range 0.91‚Äì17.54 s) |
| **K5** | Median regression rate | 0.57 (range 0.03‚Äì0.98) |
| **K6** | Median mean LHIPA | 0.04 (range 0.03‚Äì0.08) |
| **K7** | Median click position | 5.53 (range 4.01‚Äì6.89) |
| **K8** | Median mean fixations per trial | 88.6 (range 23‚Äì168) |

### Key correlations (per-participant Spearman, n = 46)

| ID | Pair | *œÅ* | *p* | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K9**  | Gaze‚Äìcursor lag √ó TTI | ‚àí0.072 | 0.632 | null |
| **K10** | Gaze‚Äìcursor lag √ó regression rate | +0.159 | 0.293 | null |
| **K11** | Gaze‚Äìcursor lag √ó LHIPA | ‚àí0.149 | 0.322 | null |
| **K12** | Regression rate √ó LHIPA | **‚àí0.568** | < 0.001 | significant ‚Äî high regressors have lower LHIPA |
| **K13** | TTI √ó Regression rate | +0.122 | 0.420 | null |
| **K14** | Click position √ó LHIPA | ‚àí0.161 | 0.285 | null |

### Chattiness √ó NB11 panel (¬ß11.5a orthogonality, n = 47)

| ID | Pair | *œÅ* range across 4 chattiness measures |
|---|---|---|
| **K15** | Chattiness √ó gaze‚Äìcursor lag | +0.03 to +0.28, all *p* > 0.06 (**orthogonal**) |
| **K16** | Chattiness √ó TTI | ‚àí0.50 to ‚àí0.57, *p* < 0.001 (chatty = faster) |
| **K17** | Chattiness √ó LHIPA | +0.34 to +0.55, *p* < 0.05 (chatty = lower cognitive load) |
| **K18** | Chattiness √ó fixations/trial | ‚àí0.41 to ‚àí0.59, *p* < 0.01 |
| **K19** | Chattiness √ó regression rate | ‚àí0.11 to ‚àí0.45 (mixed) |
| **K20** | Chattiness √ó click position | ‚àí0.12 to +0.06, null |

> **Two-factor motor structure.** K15 is the key claim: gaze‚Äìcursor lag (timing) and cursor chattiness (volume) are empirically independent individual-differences factors. Paper prose should treat them as two axes, not one.

## NB11.5: `11_5_chattiness_traits` ‚Äî cursor chattiness as a stable individual-differences trait

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/11_5_chattiness_traits.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/11_5_chattiness_traits.ipynb)*

### Chattiness trait stability (split-half reliability, n = 47)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | `events_per_sec` reliability | *r* = 0.984, Spearman‚ÄìBrown 0.992 |
| **K2** | `path_per_sec` reliability | *r* = 0.966, SB 0.983 |
| **K3** | `dir_changes_per_sec` reliability | *r* = 0.967, SB 0.983 |
| **K4** | `active_fraction` reliability | *r* = 0.966, SB 0.983 |

### Chattiness distribution (per-participant medians across 47 participants)

| ID | Measure | Median | Range | Range-√ó |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K5** | `events_per_sec` | 14.8 | 5.2 ‚Äì 55.3 | 10.6√ó |
| **K6** | `path_per_sec` (px/s) | 158.7 | 56.2 ‚Äì 469.8 | 8.4√ó |
| **K7** | `dir_changes_per_sec` | 0.608 | 0.187 ‚Äì 2.669 | 14.3√ó |
| **K8** | `active_fraction` | 0.287 | 0.132 ‚Äì 0.794 | 6.0√ó |

### LOSO M3 AUC stratified by chattiness (the deployability result)

| ID | Tercile | Median events/s | LOSO M3 AUC |
|---|---|---|---|
| **K9** | Low | 9.5 | 0.803 ¬± 0.052 (n = 15) |
| **K10** | Mid | 14.7 | 0.780 ¬± 0.065 (n = 16) |
| **K11** | High | 28.8 | 0.793 ¬± 0.064 (n = 16) |
| **K12** | Pooled LOSO M3 AUC (replication of NB21 ¬ß4.3) | ‚Äî | **0.792** (n = 47) |

| ID | Spearman | *œÅ* | *p* |
|---|---|---|---|
| **K13** | Per-participant chattiness (events/s) √ó per-participant AUC | ‚àí0.01 | 0.96 |
| **K14** | Per-participant `path_per_sec` √ó AUC | ‚àí0.06 | 0.69 |
| **K15** | Per-participant `dir_changes_per_sec` √ó AUC | ‚àí0.11 | 0.48 |
| **K16** | Per-participant `active_fraction` √ó AUC | ‚àí0.00 | 0.98 |

### Exposure-bias check (records per trial √ó chattiness)

| ID | Pair | *œÅ* | *p* |
|---|---|---|---|
| **K17** | Records per trial √ó `events_per_sec` | **‚àí0.50** | **0.0004** |
| **K18** | Records per trial √ó `active_fraction` | ‚àí0.39 | 0.007 |
| **K19** | Records per trial √ó `path_per_sec` | ‚àí0.08 | 0.59 (null) |
| **K20** | Records per trial √ó `dir_changes_per_sec` | ‚àí0.18 | 0.23 (null) |

> **Three headline claims.** (1) Chattiness is a 10√ó-range, high-reliability individual-differences trait (K1‚ÄìK4). (2) LOSO M3 AUC is flat across chattiness terciles; all 4 Spearmans are ns (K12‚ÄìK16). (3) Chatty users fixate *fewer* positions per trial, not more (K17) ‚Äî the four-class taxonomy is not inflated by parker mechanical undersampling.

<a id="nb14-14_butterworth_cognitive_load"></a>

## NB14: `14_butterworth_cognitive_load` ‚Äî cognitive load decreases with SERP position

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/14_butterworth_cognitive_load.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/14_butterworth_cognitive_load.ipynb)*

### Cognitive load decreases with SERP position (the Butterworth key finding)

**Convention.** Butterworth LF/HF ratio (Duchowski's index): *higher* LF/HF = more load. LHIPA (Index of Pupillary Activity): *lower* LHIPA = more load. The two indices are negatively correlated by construction and both agree on direction (K7).

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Trials with usable Butterworth LF/HF data | 2,719 |
| **K2** | Position-segment count (click position √ó LF/HF) | 6,874 |
| **K3** | **Position √ó median LF/HF (load DECREASES with deeper click)** | **œÅ = ‚àí0.618, *p* = 0.0426** |
| **K4** | Positions 1‚Äì10 only (excluding pos 0) | œÅ = ‚àí0.491, *p* = 0.150 (ns) |
| **K5** | Within-trial Spearman (position vs LF/HF) | N = 1,167 trials, mean œÅ = ‚àí0.105, median œÅ = ‚àí0.200, 56.6 % negative |
| **K6** | Clicked vs non-clicked median LF/HF | **22.24 (N = 1,110)** vs **19.01 (N = 5,472)**; Mann‚ÄìWhitney *U* = 3,257,823, *p* = 1.30 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å¥ ‚Äî clicked results carry more load than non-clicked |
| **K7** | Cross-index validation: trial-mean LF/HF √ó LHIPA | œÅ = ‚àí0.122, *p* = 9.29 √ó 10‚Åª¬π‚Å∞, N = 2,492 (correct sign: both indices agree on load direction) |
| **K8** | Position-level medians (load by rank) | pos 0: 29.98 (N = 1,015) ‚Üí pos 1: 21.20 ‚Üí pos 2: 18.29 ‚Üí pos 3: 16.00 ‚Üí pos 4: 16.27 ‚Ä¶ (monotone decline) |

> **Not working memory accumulation.** If prose says "forward-only dwell increases with position *consistent with working memory accumulation*," the prose is wrong. K3 shows per-fixation cognitive load *decreasing* with position ‚Äî extra dwell at deeper positions reflects allocation / comparison-set growth, not WM overload. Framework compilation, not working-memory accumulation. This is the load-bearing claim for the ETTAC 2026 and CHI 2027 framings.
>
> **Caveat on K3.** The *p* = 0.043 at the position level is borderline at Œ± = 0.05; the 1‚Äì10 subset (K4) is non-significant. The robust claim is the direction: every measurement stream (between-position LF/HF, within-trial LF/HF, median-by-position table, cross-index LHIPA) points the same way ‚Äî load declines with position.
>
> **Coordinate-space audit (2026-04-09).** `compute_butterworth_lfhf.py` previously double-counted scroll offset when deriving `click_pos` from evtrack `ypos` (already page-space). The fix moved K6 only: N_clicked 1,145 ‚Üí 1,110, clicked median 22.86 ‚Üí 22.24, *p* 3.9 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å∑ ‚Üí 1.3 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å¥. Direction and significance preserved. K1‚ÄìK5, K7, K8 are unchanged because they use fixation position (gaze ‚Üí page-space conversion, which was always correct), not `click_pos`. See `CHANGELOG.md`.

## NB15: `15_cursor_approach` ‚Äî cursor approach features and consideration set

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/15_cursor_approach.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/15_cursor_approach.ipynb)*

### Cursor approach features (per result-position record)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Records / trials / base click rate | 15,397 records / 2,340 trials / 14.4% (2,214 clicks) |
| **K2** | Mean min\_dist (clicked vs not) | 204 px vs 427 px |
| **K3** | Almost-clicked (min\_dist < 58 px, not clicked) | 734 records (4.8% of all; 5.57% of non-clicked) |
| **K4** | Approached (min\_dist < 100 px) | 2,488 records (16.2%), click rate 41.5%, lift 2.9√ó |
| **K5** | Best single-threshold AUC | min\_dist < 150 px ‚Üí AUC 0.699 |

### Approach ‚Üí regression ‚Üí click pathway

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K6** | Approach ‚Üí regression rate | 83.8% (vs 50.7% no-approach) |
| **K7** | Approach ‚Üí regression odds ratio | **5.04√ó**, p = 1.9 √ó 10‚Åª¬≤‚Å∞¬≥ |
| **K8** | Approach + regression ‚Üí click rate | **43.5%** (N = 2,086) |
| **K9** | Approach + no regression ‚Üí click rate | 30.8% (N = 402) |
| **K10** | No approach + regression ‚Üí click rate | 11.6% (N = 6,551) |
| **K11** | No approach + no regression ‚Üí click rate | 6.7% (N = 6,358) |

### Position gradient

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K12** | Position 0: almost-clicked rate | 15.5% (N = 2,310) |
| **K13** | Position 0: mean min\_dist | 170 px |
| **K14** | Position 9: almost-clicked rate | 0.0% (N = 661) |
| **K15** | Position 9: mean min\_dist | 683 px |

### Click prediction (replicates NB21 from raw features)

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K16** | Position + dwell + all approach (LOSO) | AUC 0.797 ¬± 0.021 |
| **K17** | Position coefficient (full model) | ‚àí0.380 (‚Üí skip; correct direction post-fix) |
| **K18** | direction\_changes coefficient | ‚àí0.005 (‚âà 0; was scroll artifact pre-fix) |

> **Coordinate-space audit (2026-04-09).** Two bug sites in the feature computation (`my + fix_scroll` where `my` is already page-space) inflated cursor proximity on scrolled trials (82% of corpus). Pre-fix: almost-clicked was 14%, M3 AUC was 0.827, `position` coefficient was +0.21 (wrong direction), `direction_changes` was +0.20 (scroll artifact). All values above are post-fix. See `CHANGELOG.md`.

<a id="nb21-21_click_prediction"></a>

## NB21: `21_click_prediction` ‚Äî LOSO click prediction and four-class taxonomy

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/21_click_prediction.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/21_click_prediction.ipynb)*

### LOSO click prediction ‚Äî pooled results

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Records / participants / click rate | 15,397 episodes / 47 participants / 14.4 % click rate (2,214 clicks) |
| **K2** | Records per participant | median 320, range 76‚Äì562 |
| **K3** | **M3 (position + dwell + approach) pooled LOSO AUC** | **0.792 ¬± 0.062** (47-fold) |
| **K4** | M4 (approach features only) LOSO AUC | 0.792 ¬± 0.061 |
| **K5** | M2 (position + dwell) LOSO AUC | 0.707 ¬± 0.081 |
| **K6** | M1 (position only) LOSO AUC | 0.670 ¬± 0.085 |
| **K7** | LOSO M3 AP | 0.491 ¬± 0.118 |
| **K8** | Leakage Œî (Random KFold ‚àí LOSO) for M2/M3/M4 | +0.018 / +0.006 / +0.001 (mild, direction now consistent with leakage) |
| **K9** | **Per-participant LOSO M3 AUC** | **median 0.798, IQR [0.759, 0.831], range [0.589, 0.889]** (all 47 participants above chance) |
| **K10** | Youden's J threshold (M3 OOF) | *p* = 0.495 (TPR = 0.708, FPR = 0.253) |
| **K11** | F1-optimal threshold | *p* = 0.660, F1 = 0.480 |
| **K12** | Brier score (M3 OOF) | 0.1781 |

### Four-class taxonomy (classifier-derived, tautology fix)

| ID | Class | N | % | Mean *p*(click) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K13** | Clicked | 2,214 | 14.4 % | 0.633 |
| **K14** | Deferred candidate | 1,112 | 7.2 % | 0.714 |
| **K15** | Evaluated-rejected | 344 | 2.2 % | 0.391 |
| **K16** | No signal | 11,727 | 76.2 % | 0.331 |

### M3 standardized feature coefficients (full-data refit)

| ID | Feature | Coefficient | Direction |
|---|---|---|---|
| **K17** | `mean_dist` | +0.672 | ‚Üí click |
| **K18** | `final_dist` | ‚àí0.642 | ‚Üí skip |
| **K19** | `dwell_in_proximity_ms` | +0.639 | ‚Üí click |
| **K20** | `min_dist` | ‚àí0.557 | ‚Üí skip |
| **K21** | `position` | ‚àí0.380 | ‚Üí skip |
| **K22** | `retreat_dist` | ‚àí0.172 | ‚Üí skip |
| **K23** | `mean_approach_velocity` | +0.152 | ‚Üí click |
| **K24** | `max_approach_velocity` | +0.121 | ‚Üí click |
| **K25** | `frac_decreasing` | +0.095 | ‚Üí click |
| **K26** | `total_dwell_ms` | ‚àí0.088 | ‚Üí skip |
| **K27** | `direction_changes` | ‚àí0.005 | neutral |

> **Robustness to individual cursor activity lives in NB11.5.** The chattiness-stratified AUC figure (¬ß4.3 robustness paragraph of `docs/drafts/cikm-2026/paper.md`) uses [NB11_5:K9‚ÄìK16], not NB21 directly.
>
> **Previously fixed bug (2026-04-08):** Cell 20 used `y_p_full` before definition (lines 14‚Äì15 were dead code from a pre-rewrite version). Symptom: `NameError: name 'y_p_full' is not defined`. Fix: delete the old block.
>
> **Coordinate-space audit (2026-04-09).** NB15's `compute_approach_features` double-counted scroll offset on the cursor coordinate (`my + fix_scroll` ‚Äî `my` is already page-space). Impact was concentrated on scrolled trials, which are 82 % of the corpus. Regenerating `cursor-approach-features.json` after fixing the two bug sites and re-running NB21 shifts every row above. Headline changes: click rate 12.9 % ‚Üí 14.4 % (233 correctly re-labeled clicks), M3 LOSO AUC **0.827 ‚Üí 0.792** (‚àí0.035; approach still the dominant source of signal above position+dwell), K21 (`position`) flips sign +0.21 ‚Üí ‚àí0.38 (the rank effect in the correct direction ‚Äî later positions click less), K27 (`direction_changes`) drops from +0.20 to ‚âà0 (was largely scroll artifact). Four-class Evaluated-rejected N drops 994 ‚Üí 344 because the pre-fix "approach" signal at deep positions was mostly the scroll leak. Direction of every headline result (approach > position + dwell, M4 ‚âà M3, per-participant all > 0.5) is preserved. See `CHANGELOG.md` for before/after table and NB15 feature-level diff.

<a id="nb22-22_four_class_taxonomy"></a>

## NB22: `22_four_class_taxonomy` ‚Äî regression-based four-class taxonomy and element-type interactions

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/22_four_class_taxonomy.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/22_four_class_taxonomy.ipynb)*

### Regression-based four-class taxonomy

NB22 defines the four classes via cursor approach (min_dist < 100 px) + scroll regression to that position, not via the classifier threshold used in NB21. The class definitions are complementary, not competing.

| ID | Class | N | % |
|---|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Clicked | 2,214 | 14.4 % |
| **K2** | Deferred (approached + regressed to) | 1,178 | 7.7 % |
| **K3** | Evaluated-rejected (approached + no regression) | 278 | 1.8 % |
| **K4** | Not approached | 11,727 | 76.2 % |

### Motor signature separation (deferred vs evaluated-rejected)

| ID | Metric | Deferred | Eval-Rejected | *p* |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K5** | Retreat distance (px) | 191.3 | 96.4 | 1.9 √ó 10‚Åª¬π¬π |
| **K6** | Total gaze dwell (ms) | 3,842 | 2,018 | 3.7 √ó 10‚Åª¬≤‚Å∂ |
| **K7** | Dwell in proximity (ms) | 1,219 | 682 | 5.0 √ó 10‚Åª‚Åπ |

### Click prediction with element-type interactions

| ID | Element type | N | Click% | M3 AUC | M3+interactions AUC | Œî |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K8** | organic | 12,030 | 15.1 % | 0.797 | 0.803 | +0.007 |
| **K9** | dd_top | 1,384 | 17.8 % | 0.871 | **0.899** | **+0.028** |
| **K10** | native_ad | 1,983 | 7.7 % | 0.798 | 0.786 | ‚àí0.011 |

| ID | Model | LOSO AUC |
|---|---|---|
| **K11** | M3 (replication of NB21) | 0.792 ¬± 0.062 |
| **K12** | M3 + regression feature | 0.792 ¬± 0.061 (regression adds +0.000) |

> **Interaction features help where discrimination cost is highest.** Top ads gain +0.028 AUC from element-type interactions. Native ads lose ‚àí0.011 (avoidance behavior muddies the signal). Regression features add exactly zero to any model variant ‚Äî the forward-pass approach features carry the full signal.

<a id="nb23-23_rank_effects"></a>

## NB23: `23_rank_effects` ‚Äî unified rank effects ‚Äî framework compilation

*Source: [`notebooks-v2/23_rank_effects.ipynb`](../notebooks-v2/23_rank_effects.ipynb)*

### Unified rank effects (position 0‚Äì10)

| ID | Measure | Spearman œÅ | *p* | N positions |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K1** | Click share √ó position | **‚àí0.973** | 5.1 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å∑ | 11 |
| **K2** | Fixation count √ó position | ‚àí0.442 | 0.200 (ns) | 10 |
| **K3** | Total dwell √ó position | ‚àí0.515 | 0.128 (ns) | 10 |
| **K4** | Butterworth LF/HF √ó position | **‚àí0.618** | **0.0426** | 11 |
| **K5** | LHIPA √ó click position | **‚àí0.955** | < 10‚Åª‚Åµ | 11 |

### Corpus composition

| ID | Claim | Value |
|---|---|---|
| **K6** | Trials with clicks | 2,764 |
| **K7** | (trial, position) rows | 16,335 |
| **K8** | Forward fixations (% of classified) | 74 % (150,993) |
| **K9** | Regression fixations (% of classified) | 26 % (53,583) |

### Position-level summary (per-result medians/means)

| ID | Pos | Click% | Fix count | Dwell (s) | LF/HF | LHIPA |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **K10** | 0 | 22.0 | 19.9 | 4.24 | 30.0 | 0.039 |
| **K11** | 1 | 26.9 | 14.2 | 3.18 | 21.2 | 0.039 |
| **K12** | 2 | 24.3 | 10.6 | 2.37 | 18.3 | 0.039 |
| **K13** | 3 | 11.9 | 10.0 | 2.21 | 16.0 | 0.039 |

> **Framework compilation, not declining interest.** K4 (LF/HF œÅ = ‚àí0.618) is the key result: cognitive load peaks at position 0 where the user is constructing evaluation criteria from scratch, drops steeply through positions 0‚Äì3 as criteria compile, then plateaus. K2 and K3 are non-significant at the position level (N = 10 points), but the direction is consistent: both time and effort decline. The dissociation between K2/K3 (declining time) and K4 (declining effort that drops *faster*) is the framework compilation signature.
>
> **K4 matches NB14:K3 exactly** (œÅ = ‚àí0.618, p = 0.0426) ‚Äî same data, independent computation path. Cross-notebook replication.

## Regenerating this file

```bash
cd ~/Documents/dev/attentional-foraging/notebooks-v2
.venv/bin/python update_key_claims.py
```

The script is idempotent: it updates every notebook's Key Claims block in place (replacing any existing block by its marker line) and regenerates this aggregate document. Notebook execution state is not touched ‚Äî only the Key Claims markdown cell. Re-run the script any time a canonical number changes.
